hypothesis:
Given that the paper demonstrates superior performance of single-agent approaches with structured preprocessing over both baseline LLMs and multi-agent systems, yet identifies persistent failures in extracting chemical structures (SMILES) from molecular images across all tested methods, I propose:

**Hypothesis:** A hybrid extraction pipeline that integrates specialized molecular image segmentation models (trained on annotated chemical structure datasets) upstream of LLM-based agents will achieve >80% precision and recall on SMILES extraction from scientific articles, significantly outperforming current single-agent approaches which score near-zero on this task, by decoupling the computer vision challenge of molecular localization from the semantic reasoning tasks at which LLMs excel.
